intro: Angelica sinensis tea, also known as dong quai, is a traditional Chinese herbal remedy that has been used for centuries to promote women's health and well-being. Made from the dried root of the Angelica sinensis plant, this tea is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to alleviate menstrual cramps, reduce stress, and improve overall vitality. Regular consumption of angelica sinensis tea is believed to promote a healthy balance of hormones and support the body's natural healing processes.
side_effects: Angelica sinensis tea can have side effects such as increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties, gastrointestinal upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. It may also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications. Pregnant and breastfeeding women should consult their healthcare provider before consuming this tea due to potential risks.
uses: Angelica sinensis tea is used to aid in reducing inflammation and improving circulation, while also helping to alleviate menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. It is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. Additionally, angelica sinensis tea may help to boost the immune system and reduce fever.
heavy_periods: Angelica sinensis tea, also known as dong quai, is a herbal remedy that has been traditionally used to help alleviate heavy menstrual periods due to its purported ability to regulate and balance the body's energy and menstrual cycles. Some studies suggest that it may help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by promoting uterine health and improving blood flow. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications or health conditions.
fever: Angelica sinensis tea, also known as dong quai, is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to help alleviate fever and other symptoms associated with respiratory infections. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce body temperature and promote overall well-being. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using angelica sinensis tea as a treatment for fever, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
recipe: To make angelica sinensis tea for medicinal use, first boil 8-10 ounces of water and then add 5-7 grams of dried angelica sinensis root. Steep the root for 5-7 minutes, allowing the water to infuse with the active compounds. After straining the tea, it is recommended to drink 1-2 cups per day to potentially alleviate symptoms related to menstrual cramps and other health issues.
taste: Angelica sinensis tea is often described as having a sweet and slightly bitter taste, with a hint of earthy undertones. The flavor profile can vary depending on the quality and processing of the tea leaves. Some people may also detect a subtle numbing or cooling sensation on the tongue after consuming angelica sinensis tea.
pregancy: Angelica sinensis tea, also known as dong quai tea, is not typically recommended for pregnant women as it may stimulate the uterus and lead to premature labor. However, some studies suggest that when taken in moderation, it may help alleviate symptoms of pregnancy such as nausea and dizziness. It's essential for pregnant women to consult their healthcare provider before consuming any herbal tea, including angelica sinensis tea.

blend recipe: Angelica sinensis tea is a traditional Chinese herbal blend often combined with other herbs such as ginseng and licorice root to enhance its medicinal properties. To make this tea, combine 1 teaspoon of dried Angelica sinensis with 1 teaspoon of ginseng and 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root in a tea infuser. Steep the herbal blend in boiling water for 5-7 minutes before straining and serving.

dosage: Angelica sinensis tea is typically consumed in small quantities, with a standard dosage ranging from 0.5 to 2 grams of dried root per 8-ounce cup of tea.
